picks.rationale: The Vegas Golden Knights have demonstrated strong performance against the spread, covering in six consecutive games and holding an 8-2 ATS record in their last 10 contests. They secured a 5-4 victory in Game 1, indicating their ability to keep games tight or win outright even on the road. Vegas has won all three head-to-head matchups against Carolina this season, further supporting their capability to stay within a one-goal margin. The stability of the +1.5 line despite their Game 1 win suggests the market anticipates another closely contested game.

All three head-to-head meetings between the Golden Knights and Hurricanes this season have gone over 5.5 goals, with final totals of 7, 7, and 9 goals. Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final continued this trend, ending in a high-scoring 5-4 affair. Both teams boast potent offenses and elite power-play units, with Vegas at 24.47% and Carolina at 24.90%, which can quickly drive up the score. Additionally, both teams rank highly in third-period goal scoring, suggesting offensive action will persist throughout the game.

The Carolina Hurricanes are known for their ability to bounce back from losses, holding the NHL's best regular-season points percentage (.759) in such scenarios. Playing on home ice after a Game 1 defeat, they will be highly motivated to even the series. Carolina outshot Vegas in Game 1 and, despite their 0-3 H2H record against the Golden Knights this season, their overall dominant regular season (53-22-7) and strong home record (29-10-2) position them for a strong performance.

The Vegas Golden Knights enter Game 2 with significant momentum, having won seven consecutive playoff games, including their road victory in Game 1 of the Stanley Cup Final. They have also won all three head-to-head matchups against the Carolina Hurricanes this season, demonstrating a consistent ability to defeat them. Vegas's depth scoring, with players like Brett Howden leading the playoffs in goals and Tomas Hertl scoring the Game 1 winner, provides multiple offensive threats. Their composure in overcoming deficits further strengthens their case for an outright win.

As the top seed in the Eastern Conference with a 53-22-7 regular season record and a formidable 29-10-2 home record, the Carolina Hurricanes are expected to deliver a strong response in Game 2. Following a surprising Game 1 loss where their goalie Frederik Andersen struggled, a return to his elite playoff form is anticipated. Coach Rod Brind'Amour is likely to emphasize their strong defensive structure and forechecking pressure, and if Carolina executes their game plan effectively, a multi-goal victory on home ice to even the series is a plausible outcome.

The Atlanta Braves are in excellent form (42-20) and have already secured the first two games of this series against the struggling Toronto Blue Jays (29-33), who have lost four straight. Atlanta ace Chris Sale (8-3, 2.01 ERA, 0.94 WHIP) is scheduled to pitch, while Toronto has an unconfirmed starter, creating a significant pitching mismatch. The Braves are looking to complete a sweep at home, and getting them at -1.5 for -114 offers strong value given their superior form and pitching advantage.

The total for this game opened at 9 (even money) and has seen significant reverse line movement, shifting to 9 Over (-117) / Under (-103) despite 100% of public money on the Over. This indicates sharp money is heavily backing the Under. Royals starter Seth Lugo (2-4, 3.57 ERA) has a strong home-run suppression rate, which is a key factor in keeping the score low. Trusting the sharp money and Lugo's ability to limit runs makes the Under 9 a high-confidence play.

Both starting pitchers, Trevor Rogers (2-6, 6.84 ERA) for the Orioles and Brayan Bello (2-5, 5.63 ERA) for the Red Sox, have struggled significantly this season. The Boston Red Sox offense is particularly strong against left-handed pitching, ranking seventh in MLB in OPS, which bodes poorly for the left-handed Rogers. While the verified total of 11.5 is high, the market has already shown a strong lean towards the over, with the total moving up from 10 to 10.5 with 100% of both dollars and tickets on the over. This combination of poor pitching and capable offenses, especially against a lefty, points to a high-scoring affair.

The Los Angeles Dodgers have a dominant head-to-head record against the Arizona Diamondbacks, winning 8 of their last 9 matchups and 17 of their last 29 games. The Dodgers also secured a decisive 7-0 victory in yesterday's game, demonstrating their current superiority. Furthermore, the Diamondbacks are significantly hampered by injuries, with numerous key players including Lourdes Gurriel Jr. (hamstring) and James McCann (quadricep) on the injured list. This combination of H2H dominance, recent form, and injury advantage makes the Dodgers moneyline a strong pick.

The Chicago Cubs are currently in poor form, holding a 1-4 record in their last five games and losing to the Athletics yesterday. The Cubs are also dealing with several significant pitching injuries, including Justin Steele (left elbow) on the 60-day IL. While the Athletics are not a dominant team, their recent win against the Cubs and Chicago's struggles and injuries suggest they can keep this game close. Taking the Athletics at +1.5 runs provides a good cushion against a struggling home team.
